about_title:
POZA DE LA ARENILLA
COASTAL WETLAND
about_history_p1: The Poza de La Arenilla Coastal Wetland is located on the southern coast of the La Punta district, occupying an area of 18.2 hectares. It is an artificial environment of semi-stagnant coastal waters, originated by the construction of two breakwaters that protect the southern shore of the district, which allowed the creation of a quiet water zone where various aquatic species began to inhabit, developing the characteristics of a typical wetland. Water circulation in the Poza de La Arenilla depends mainly on tidal changes and, to a lesser extent, on wind direction and intensity.
about_history_p2: This area has become a refuge for various aquatic species, including migratory and resident birds, fish, crustaceans, and plants, which find in Poza de La Arenilla an ideal space to reproduce, feed, and rest. In addition to its ecological value, it constitutes a place of scientific and tourist interest.
about_biodiv_p1: Our tourist resource has a total of 104 registered bird species, between residents and migratory. It is also home to 56 species of fish, 21 of which are warm water and 6 types of crustaceans. Because of its size and the biodiversity found in it, the Poza de La Arenilla Coastal Wetland is the smallest in the Callao region; however, it concentrates the largest amount of flora and avifauna. This makes it a unique space for bird watching and studying coastal ecosystems in an urban area.
about_tourism_p1: Since 1999, the District Municipality of La Punta protects the Poza de La Arenilla Coastal Wetland as a Reserved Zone and is responsible for its conservation, preservation, and tourism promotion. It also encourages educational activities and guided tours in more than two languages, to highlight its ecological value and biodiversity, attracting tourists interested in nature.
btn3Sub: Discover La Arenilla and its history
scan_instruction: Take a photo or select an image from your gallery to identify a wetland species
scan_analyzing_hint: This may take a few seconds
scan_not_in_db: This species is not in our wetland database, but the AI suggests an identification
scan_no_internet: Internet connection required to identify species
no_audio: Audio not available for this species
home_description: Explore and protect the biodiversity of La Arenilla Wetland.

playero_arenero.name: Sanderling
playero_arenero.description: A small, highly active shorebird known for running tirelessly along the shoreline chasing marine invertebrates. It is a migratory species that breeds in the Arctic and arrives on the Peruvian coast during the boreal winter.
playero_arenero.habitat: Open sandy beaches, estuaries, and marshes.
playero_arenero.diet: Small crustaceans, mollusks, and marine invertebrates washed up by the waves.

chorlo_semipalmeado.name: Semipalmated Plover
chorlo_semipalmeado.description: A small migratory shorebird characterized by a dark band across its chest and a short, thick bill. It feeds on mudflats and beaches, running quickly for short distances before stopping suddenly to look for prey.

playerito_semipalmado.name: Semipalmated Sandpiper
playerito_semipalmado.description: One of the most common and smallest sandpipers. During migration, it forms huge synchronized flocks. It has black legs and feeds by rapidly probing shallow mud and sand with its short bill.
playerito_semipalmado.diet: Aquatic invertebrates, small mollusks, and amphipods.

zarapito_trinador.name: Whimbrel
zarapito_trinador.description: A large shorebird, easily recognized by its long, downward-curving bill and distinctive striped head pattern. Its call is a characteristic rapid, whistling trill heard over long distances.
zarapito_trinador.diet: Crabs, snails, marine worms, and insects.

gaviota_peruana.name: Belcher's Gull
gaviota_peruana.description:
It is a medium-sized marine bird endemic to the South American Pacific coast, especially in Peru and Chile. Its plumage is gray on the upper part, with a white head and dark details during mating season; its beak is yellow with a red band, and its legs are yellow, making it easily identifiable.

Coastal Habitats: It lives on rocky coasts and sandy beaches, feeding on fish, mollusks, crustaceans, and even urban waste. Its varied and opportunistic diet allows it to adapt to different food sources.

Life Cycle and Reproduction: It is social and forms large groups, but becomes territorial during the breeding season between November and March. It nests in rocky areas and uninhabited islands, laying one to three light brown eggs with dark spots for camouflage.

Urgent Conservation: Despite being resilient, it faces serious threats from human activity, such as marine pollution, overfishing, and unregulated tourism.

Conservation and Future: It is crucial to implement environmental education programs, reduce marine pollution, and promote sustainable fishing to ensure its survival.
gaviota_peruana.habitat: Rocky coasts, sandy beaches, and urban areas of the South American Pacific.
gaviota_peruana.diet: Fish, mollusks, crustaceans, and urban waste.

gaviota_franklin.name: Franklin's Gull
gaviota_franklin.description: An elegant gull, famous for migrating thousands of kilometers from the North American prairies to the South American Pacific coast. During the austral summer, thousands of these gulls invade the beaches. Recognized by their black hood.
gaviota_franklin.habitat: Lakes, inland wetlands, and coastal beaches.
gaviota_franklin.diet: Insects, earthworms, small fish, and invertebrates.

cormoran_neotropical.name: Neotropic Cormorant
cormoran_neotropical.description:
It is an aquatic bird species inhabiting fresh and saltwater bodies across the Americas, from the southern US to northern Chile and Argentina. Easily recognizable by its shiny black plumage that can show a greenish or bluish tint in the right light. It has blue-green eyes and a long, thin beak with a hooked tip, adapted for catching fish. It measures between 60 and 75 cm.

Fishing Master: It is an expert predator in fishing, thanks to its agility underwater. It uses a diving technique, swimming rapidly to chase its prey with the help of its webbed feet.

Sun-drying Ritual: After fishing, it can be seen on the shore with its wings spread out to dry. Unlike other aquatic birds, cormorants lack well-developed oil glands to waterproof their feathers, so they must sun-dry to fly efficiently.

Health Indicator: Its presence is a sign of a balanced ecosystem. However, pollution and habitat destruction negatively impact their populations.

Symbol of Adaptation: It is a fascinating example of adaptation to diverse environments. In some cultures, it has been the subject of myths and legends.
cormoran_neotropical.habitat: Fresh and saltwater bodies, mangroves, rivers, estuaries, and lakes across the Americas.

piquero_peruano.name: Peruvian Booby
piquero_peruano.description: An iconic seabird of the Peruvian coast. It gathers in immense flocks and dives spectacularly from great heights to catch fish. It has brownish-gray legs and a mottled white and brown back.
piquero_peruano.habitat: Coastal ocean, rocky cliffs, and marine islands.
piquero_peruano.diet: Peruvian anchoveta, sardines, and pelagic fish.

garza_blanca_grande.name: Great Egret
garza_blanca_grande.description: One of the tallest and most elegant wading birds. Entirely white with an imposing yellow bill and long black legs. It waits patiently motionless in shallow water until it launches a rapid harpoon strike with its neck to catch its prey.
garza_blanca_grande.habitat: Lakes, wetlands, riverbanks, marshes, and swamps.
garza_blanca_grande.diet: Fish, frogs, snakes, large crustaceans, and small rodents.

ostrero_americano.name: American Oystercatcher
ostrero_americano.description: An unmistakable and noisy coastal bird, with a black head, brown back, and pure white belly. Its most prominent feature is its very long, strong, bright orange-red bill, which it uses as the perfect tool to open bivalve mollusks.
ostrero_americano.habitat: Rocky coasts, sandy and muddy beaches, islets.
ostrero_americano.diet: Oysters, mussels, limpets, and other hard-shelled invertebrates.

garza_azul.name: Little Blue Heron
garza_azul.description:
It is an aquatic bird of the Ardeidae family, inhabiting coastal and swampy areas of the Americas, especially in tropical and subtropical climates. It is recognized by its slender figure, long legs, and neck. Adults have slate-blue plumage, while juveniles are initially white and change color as they mature.

Fishing Strategies: It feeds mainly on fish, crustaceans, and aquatic insects, as well as small amphibians and reptiles. It uses a meticulous hunting technique, moving slowly or remaining motionless to catch its prey quickly.

Reproduction: It breeds in colonies, often alongside other aquatic birds, building nests in trees or shrubs near the water. Females lay three to five eggs, and both parents collaborate in incubation.

Threats & Conservation: Despite its adaptability, it faces threats from habitat destruction, pollution, and urban expansion. Protecting wetlands is crucial for its sustainability.
garza_azul.habitat: Coastal and swampy areas of the Americas, tropical and subtropical climates.
garza_azul.diet: Fish, crustaceans, aquatic insects, small amphibians, and reptiles.

pelicano_peruano.name: Peruvian Pelican
pelicano_peruano.description:
It is a distinctive species of the South American Pacific coasts, from northern Chile to southern Ecuador. It is recognized by its large size, dark plumage with white and gray tones, and its characteristic white crest. Its long colorful beak with an extensible pouch makes it unique.

Master of fishing: The Peruvian pelican is famous for its plunge-diving technique, capturing anchovies and small fish in its pouch by diving from great heights.

Natural Thermometer: It plays a crucial ecological role, acting as an indicator of marine health. In times of abundance, they form large colonies of thousands, but during El Niño their population decreases due to fish migration.

Family Cycle: Mating season varies according to food availability. Females lay two to three eggs, which they incubate for a month. Both parents care for the chicks.

Protection: It faces serious threats from pollution, overfishing, and climate change. The conservation of this species is essential to maintain ecological balance on the coasts.
pelicano_peruano.habitat: South American Pacific coasts, marine ecosystems.
pelicano_peruano.diet: Anchovies and small fish, captured by diving.

infoText: La Arenilla Wetland is home to diverse species and a unique ecosystem we must conserve.
terms_read_hint: Please read all terms before accepting.
terms_scroll_hint: Scroll to the bottom to accept
terms_s16_body:
Participants may not use real personal data, sensitive, secret, reserved, confidential information, credentials, keys, tokens, internal endpoints, records, non-public institutional databases or protected information, unless expressly, formally and previously authorized by the competent entity and in accordance with applicable regulations.

Priority will be given to the use of:
terms_s16_footer: The sponsoring entities, through their digital innovation laboratory team, will be responsible for verifying that information shared during the Hackathon does not violate legal, administrative, contractual, security or data protection restrictions.
terms_s17_body:
The use of generative artificial intelligence tools will be permitted solely for purposes of supporting development, documentation, design, analysis, ideation or prototype improvement.

It is prohibited to enter the following into such tools:
terms_s17_b: Confidential, secret, reserved or sensitive information.
terms_s17_c: Credentials, keys, tokens or access mechanisms.
terms_s17_f: Source code, configurations or architecture of unauthorized production systems.
terms_s17_footer: Teams must declare the use of generative AI tools when applicable and will be responsible for reviewing, validating and assuming responsibility for the content, results or components incorporated in their solutions.
terms_s18_body:
Participants, mentors, judges and other actors involved must maintain confidentiality regarding non-public information they may access during the Hackathon.

The confidentiality obligation remains in effect even after the Hackathon has concluded.

It is prohibited to disclose, reproduce, publish, transfer, store in public repositories, upload to external platforms or use for purposes other than the Hackathon any unauthorized institutional information.
terms_s18_footer: Failure to comply with this obligation may result in disqualification, exclusion from the process, withdrawal of recognition and other actions as appropriate under applicable regulations.
terms_s19_body:
Participants must observe minimum digital security rules.

The following are prohibited:
terms_s19_a: Interfering with real systems of public entities.
terms_s19_b: Scanning, breaching, testing or exploiting systems without express authorization.
terms_s19_d: Publishing keys, tokens, internal endpoints or sensitive configurations.
terms_s19_e: Incorporating malware, spyware, mechanisms for improper information extraction or malicious code.
terms_s19_f: Using data from capture, intrusion or exploitation on public or private infrastructure without formal authorization.
terms_s19_footer: Solutions must be developed in test, simulated or controlled environments.
terms_s20_body:
The SGTD-PCM may collect personal data from participants for registration, admissibility, evaluation, communications, organization, issuance of certificates, dissemination of results, systematization, knowledge management and subsequent articulation with public digital innovation initiatives.

Data may include names, surnames, identity documents, email, phone, profile, institution of origin, declared experience, image, voice and other information necessary for Hackathon management.

The processing of personal data will be carried out in accordance with applicable personal data protection regulations.

Participants accept the processing of their personal data for the purposes described by submitting their application and accepting these rules.
terms_s21_title: Use of image, voice and institutional dissemination
terms_s21_body:
Participation in the Hackathon implies authorization for the use of image, voice, name, team, profile, entity or institution of origin, name of the solution and audiovisual records generated during the activity.

Such authorization is granted in favor of the PCM, the SGTD-PCM and, where applicable, the participating public entities, exclusively for institutional purposes, dissemination of results, accountability, public communication, knowledge management, academic purposes and promotion of public digital innovation.
terms_s21_footer: The use of image and voice will not have commercial purposes nor will it generate the right to economic compensation.
terms_intro_title: ArenIa respects your privacy and complies with current regulations.
terms_intro_body: This application was developed for educational and conservation purposes. Below we clearly explain how we operate and why you can use it with full confidence.
terms_c1_title: We do not collect personal data
terms_c1_body: ArenIa does not request, store or process any personal user data. No registration, name, email or any other identifiable data is required to use the app.
terms_c2_title: We do not access confidential information
terms_c2_body: The app does not access institutional databases, records, internal systems or any type of sensitive, secret or reserved information. It only uses open and public data.
terms_c3_body: The artificial intelligence tools used in developing this app operated exclusively with open and reference data. Personal data, credentials or confidential information were never entered into these tools.
terms_c4_body: All content in the app — images, species descriptions, habitats and ecological data — comes from freely accessible sources, open data or was created for scientific outreach purposes.
terms_c5_title: Does not interfere with external systems
terms_c5_body: ArenIa is an informational app. It performs no scans, intrusions, unauthorized connections or interactions with systems or infrastructure of public or private entities.
terms_c6_title: No commercial use of image or voice
terms_c6_body: The app does not record, store or use the user's image, voice or biometric data. Species photographs are in the public domain or for educational use without commercial purposes.
terms_footer_note: By continuing, you confirm that you have read and understood this declaration. ArenIa was developed within the framework of the Transformagob 2026 Hackathon, in compliance with all applicable rules and regulations.
terms_body: By using this app, you agree to our terms...
compliance_body: This application complies with data protection laws...
